title: Narrow vs General AI
concept: Every AI today is narrow — brilliant at one task. General AI that can do anything a human can does not exist yet.

exercises.prompt: Recap: which term is the broadest?
exercises.explain: AI is the broadest — ML and Deep Learning live inside it.

cards.title: Today's AI is a specialist
cards.body: Every AI you've ever used does one thing well — and is hopeless at everything else. That's Narrow AI.

cards.title: Narrow AI
cards.body: Narrow AI (or "weak AI") is built for a single task: spotting spam, recommending songs, recognizing faces. A chess AI can crush a grandmaster but can't make you breakfast.

cards.title: General AI (AGI)
cards.body:
General AI would match a human across any task — learning, reasoning, adapting to anything.

It does not exist yet. It's a research goal, not a product.

cards.title: Movies vs reality
cards.body: The all-knowing robots of sci-fi are General AI. Real systems — even ChatGPT — are still narrow: powerful, but task-bound.

exercises.prompt: What kind of AI exists today?

options.text: General AI that can do anything
Narrow AI, built for specific tasks

exercises.explain: Everything in use today is Narrow AI — specialized for particular tasks.

exercises.prompt: True or false: a world-champion chess AI could also drive your car.
exercises.explain: False. A chess AI is narrow — it only plays chess. Driving needs a completely different, separate system.

exercises.sentence: AI that matches humans at any task is called ___ AI.
exercises.explain: General AI (AGI) would be a true all-rounder — and it doesn't exist yet.

exercises.prompt: Someone claims "ChatGPT is basically General AI." Best response?

options.text: "Yes — it can do literally anything a human can."
"Not quite — it's still narrow: great with text, but it can't truly do every human task."

exercises.explain: Spot on. ChatGPT is impressively broad for a language tool, but it's still Narrow AI — not a general, do-anything mind.
